reviews.review_msg: Lisa is a passionate and knowledgeable professor. While word around campus and here is negative, I've had nothing but great classes with her. Helpful, informative and interesting lectures, and a great marker with good feedback. She's friendly, but if you are annoying or say dumb things, she will let you know. She's now Dean at McGill
she doesn't even know what she's teaching. does a very poor job of explaining the material.

Lisa doesn't lecture well, nor does she explain key concepts well at all. Rambles and gets off topic a lot. Rude personality and acts condescendingly towards her students. I would absolutely never take a course with her again. Avoid her if you can.
very very good. awesome hair, moist and lustrous. Does not suffer fools gladly. Lazy fools give bad reviews - get off your suff and think!

If it wasn't for the shrill voice, the "I know everything and you know nothing" attitude, it wouldn't be so bad. But it's just that bad. She just wasn't meant to be an instructor. Or anything else for that matter.
Contrary to popular belief, there isn't anything wrong with Lisa Shapiro.  Although there isn't really anything particularly "right" about her either.  Pretty neutral.

Not sure what ppl r saying, she is so far the best phil prof. cares about students trying to make it in phil; tells student what 2 do with the discipline. papers were fun if u attended lecture. she finds other ways for those quiet ppl  (who actually do work, but do not speak in lectures) to participate. A lot of readings, but thats philosophy.

Material is deeply interesting and she allows for people to share their ideas and participate in class, but knows when to cut people off.  I think this is a really great skill for a phil prof to have.  Fair marker.  Might spend a little too long explaining some ideas, but that could also just be in virtue of the fact that this was a 200lvl class.
Avoid her class if possible.
There are certain things you just shouldn't do in life. Don't do cocaine, don't drink and drive, don't play in traffic, and don't take a class from Lisa Shapiro.
Lecture presentations on class information is very unclear.
borrrrringg! she was very monotone and i could not pay attention to her at all! and the material was really abstract and weird.. didnt like it at all

Her "standards" are said to be "high", but actually they are just obscure and qualitatively different from the rest of the department. Those who get the very highest marks in other classes get the worst ones in hers, and people who are mediocre at best in other classes thrive in Shapiro's classes. I asked around. If you are a simpleton, this is you
She knows her stuff.  She has high standards, which is refreshing at SFU.

one of the worst profs i have ever had! she rambles on and on during lectures and she doesnt seem to relate much of it to the notes. learned more off the slides then i did in class.. avoid if possible.

oh my god, i really don't know where to start. She is so boring. The things she says in class are pointless! She likes to talk about things you have access to. Talks really slow and doesnt focus on aspects we should know. I think you should just consider this course a distance ed, stay home and read the powerpoints! Waste of a professor!

omg, this prof is so boring. I'm pretty sure she's a nice person but still her lectures are so pointless. she repeats for like half an hour. And her TAs are kinda bad too. They're not helpful at all. I think i failed this course. I'm gonna retake with another teacher -_-

Learned more about Philosophy listening to podcasts on the internet during lecture. Entire course is just memorizing a bunch of philosopher's theories.There's better profs and courses to take if you just need the credits.

My advice to anyone taking a course from Lisa is to take Richard Pryor's tips on phil papers very seriously (the assumptions about the reader). She's serious about clarity over creativity. Makes sense--she does history of phil, not phil. Classes are funny! It's true she's way out of line in rudeness. So? Suck it up or complain to the dean.

The lectures are actually pretty good. Just don't discredit the complaints on here as being only from idiots who always fail. I assumed that, and took her anyway. I have serious regrets. Marks are 2 full grades lower than my usual, with full effort. Attempts to understand why and improve are met with disrespect bordering on abusive and cruel. Why?
worst.  prof.  ever.  scary, mean and dismissive.  hard marker.  avoid!
Her voice and face could curdle fresh milk, and her comments on papers suggested she is incapable of liking anything any student could ever produce.

Aw, c'mon now, Shapiro is hilarious.  I laughed a lot in her course.  I just wouldn't recommend her if you used to being snuggled through difficult problems.  Grow a backbone and really squeeze your mind grapes and it's an easy C.  Just kidding.  (Not really).

Lisa is rude.  She makes faces when students ask questions and her marking is harsh.  She does know her stuff, but I think the fact that she knows what she's talking about has gone to her head and made her unpleasant.  She has really offended lots of my classmates with her attitude.
Honestly, worst teacher I've ever had!

People have to get over that she's from NY. She's a little abrasive its true. But she's also super nice and helpful if you visit her for office hours. And she is really smart, and as a woman I find her encouraging to me in my philosophical pursuits. Just don't talk for the sake of talking, ask intelligent questions, and you'll keep your hide intact

She tries, but she really doesn't know how to communicate the ideas to students new to phil Unless you are innately familiar with E & M, this class may be difficult for you. She marks fairly, but I think it is equally important one is taught in a way that one can really understand what is going on.

Full of contempt, dismissive and totally allergic to original ideas. Not only she does not have a clue about what she is talking, she does not have a clue about what you (as a student) are saying. Fails to understand most of the philosophical concepts; very limited philosophical insight.
No Comments
Very cool - smart and clear. Crusty manner, but nice if you can accept that's just her way. I'd definitely take another class with her

This prof can teach- she just assumes you are as smart as her.. Remind her you're not! Yes, I agree she doesn't put you at ease initally, but she's very nice, very intelligent, witty, just try to show her some respect. (don't fall asleep and then wake up and ask her what she was talking about)

I liked her. She's not a people person, I agree, threw a few hissy fits (don't, for the love of mike, leave your cell phone on), and can be sarcastic and unfriendly. She's also *extremely* clever, however, and crystal clear - knows her Descartes backwards and forwards. Very impressive. Worth taking.
I believe Descartes just rolled over in his grave.
No Comments
Very nice prof, easy course and easy exams
About as beatiful on the inside as outside.

Eviiiiiil, don't take this course unless you want to be permanently discouraged for Descartes. Doesn't know what she's talking about and gets enraged at people who ask questions. She's condescending and is exactly the type of person that shouldn't teach.
I'm a third year philosophy student who had to take one of the lower requirements.  She was condescending and original thought was unwelcome.
non-stop erm...ah..erm..ah...erm....erm makes the class super boring. exams are not tough.  use her notes.
heck; no idea what she's talkin' about... Philosophy drives me crazy.
Tests are pretty easy.  She gives you the exam essay questions in advance.  She takes the time to answer people's questions in class so that is good.

I love philosophy, but this course has totally turned me off to it. Her lectures are dull, boring, and dry. Nothing really makes you think. Tests are pretty straight forward. But i learn absolutely nothing from taking this course. Waste of time and money!
Super boring lecture, notes are good, but leave too much unanswer questions. Midterms and final are not tough, just need to read all the notes and that's it

Easy tests. If you don't read, you can easily study by reading notes from sparknotes.com and you can do well enough. Notes are long and have many questions. The answers are provided in lecture. So you have to listen in lecture even though it's boring!

This course is difficult.But she helps me make sense of it. Don't blame her as a boring professor. This is Phil, how fun can it be? She has the excellent teaching skill and lots of experience. Thank you , Lisa!

Fairly easy exams, more or less tells you what is going to be one it.  However, you may feel the need to gouge your eyes out with spoons after sitting in her lecture.  Public speaking classes should be required for profs to take before lecturing.

Beware...this class is pretty boring. Dont bother buying books, everything you need to know is on the overheads. Oh and make sure you turn off your cell phone-Shapiro cant stand it when they go off in her class.
Her exams are really easy.  Even for a person like me who have no logic whatsoever, I get a pretty good mark.
what was she talking about? she totally tuned me off philosophy and we were always behind in the reading becasue she can't explain things clearly.
Arrogant mannerisms and an apparent lack of interest in teach. Tough but fair grading.
No Comments
nice prof.. clear lecture...exams are fair...study hard and you will get a good mark
No Comments
tells u whats exactly on the exam, no trick questions, pretty easy

She was great - grading was very tough, but very fair. Will make you work hard for your grade. Thinking for yourself is mandatory in her classes and papers. Knowledgeable about the subject. Will give extensions if you ask nicely.

I have never had a teacher so arrogant before, with such obvious and utter disregard for her students. It is evident that she doesn't love teaching but rather is using it as a means to do research. The least she could do is try to hide it.
I thought lectures were very clear, despite voice and sometimes patrionizing manner - grading was awful.  We can't all be as perfect as she is...

voice is EXTREMELY annoying! w/ her nasal  right?  every 5 min, my mind is ready to explode! lectures are kinda hard to follow. Seems like she's talking about a whole bunch of useless stuff, but you never know if she actually has a point or not. BAD
she's not that bad, just talks a bit too fast. Coz what she says are more useful than her notes.
she is bad bad bad bad bad bad....dunno understand at all..she sucks at teaching..
amazing how entirely useless a person can be in front of a class.  Take my advice and just use the library.  Not worth the tuition.
she is redundant redundant, has atrocious grammar, and runs lecture like grade 2
Her voice is like pavement being scratched with a metal rake
